scope_note: QA SL(2,Z) Equivariance cert [300]. Primary sources: Hardy, G.H. and Wright, E.M. (2008) An Introduction to the Theory of Numbers, Oxford, ISBN 978-0-19-921986-5, Ch.V (gcd properties, Euclidean algorithm) and Ch.X (SL(2,Z) generators L,R); Hestenes, D. and Sobczyk, G. (1984) Clifford Algebra to Geometric Calculus, Reidel, ISBN 978-90-277-1673-6 (versor sandwich product, equivariance). CLAIM (narrow, falsifiable): (C1) L-equivariance: gcd(b, b+e)=gcd(b,e) for all positive integers b,e (algebraic identity gcd(b,b+e)=gcd(b,e) because b+e=b+e and gcd(b,e) divides b+e; verified exhaustively all 81 states in {1,...,9}^2). (C2) R-equivariance: gcd(b+e, e)=gcd(b,e) similarly; verified exhaustively. (C3) Full SL(2,Z) equivariance: since L and R generate SL(2,Z) (cert [294]) and both preserve gcd (C1,C2), ALL W in {L,R}* preserve gcd(W*[b,e]^T)=gcd(b,e); verified for all 81 states under all words of length 1..8. (C4) Versor sandwich preserves char poly: L*M*L^{-1}=[[8,1],[8,2]] mod 9 and R*M*R^{-1}=[[1,1],[1,0]] mod 9 (explicit integer computation); both have char poly x^2-x-1 (same as M from cert [299]); det=-1 and trace=1 preserved. (C5) Versor sandwich preserves orbit partition: L*M*L^{-1} and R*M*R^{-1} each produce orbit partition 1+8+72 on {1,...,9}^2 with periods 1/8/24 (same as M). Mechanism: Theorem NT maintained; all computations on integer pairs. Builds on cert [294] (L,R generate SL(2,Z)), cert [298] (v3(gcd) T-invariant), cert [299] (char poly x^2-x-1). Lineage: derived 2026-06-02.
